text_segment: Plants are very important to animals. They provide food for animals to eat. Plants also give animals oxygen to breathe and shelter to live in or hide from predators.
question: Explain how plants help animals survive.
Active: Plants provide food, oxygen, and shelter for animals.

text_segment: Animals are also important to plants. Bees and butterflies pollinate flowers so plants can make seeds. Animals spread seeds to new places and their waste adds nutrients to the soil.
question: Explain how animals help plants grow.
Active: Animals pollinate flowers, spread seeds, and fertilize soil.
Progressive: Animals are pollinating flowers, spreading seeds, and fertilizing soil.

text_segment: Some plants are protected because they are at risk. Plants become endangered when their habitat is destroyed or when people pick too many of them. Climate change also threatens many plant species.

text_segment: A food chain shows how energy moves from one living thing to another. It starts with plants that make their own food from sunlight. Animals eat plants, and then other animals eat those animals.
question: Explain what a food chain is.
Active: A food chain shows how energy passes between living things.
Progressive: A food chain is showing how energy passes between living things.
Passive: Energy flow is shown by a food chain.
Subordinate: Because energy transfers through eating, food chains form.

order_subordinate_beginner_1: because,energy transfers,through eating,,,food chains,form,.
order_subordinate_beginner_2: food chains,form,because,energy transfers,through eating,.

text_segment: A land food chain might go like this: grass grows using sunlight, a grasshopper eats the grass, a frog eats the grasshopper, and a snake eats the frog. Each step passes energy along.

Active: Grass feeds grasshoppers, which feed frogs, which feed snakes.
Progressive: Grass is feeding grasshoppers, which are feeding frogs and snakes.
Passive: Grasshoppers are eaten by frogs, which are eaten by snakes.
Subordinate: After grass grows, grasshoppers eat it and frogs eat them.

order_subordinate_beginner_2: grasshoppers eat it,and frogs eat them,after,grass grows,.

text_segment: A water food chain works the same way. Algae grows in the water, tiny zooplankton eat the algae, small fish eat the zooplankton, and big fish eat the small fish.

Progressive: Algae is feeding zooplankton, which are feeding fish.
Passive: Zooplankton are eaten by small fish, which are eaten by big fish.
Subordinate: After algae grows, zooplankton eat it and fish eat them.

text_segment: Plants are called producers because they make their own food. They use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to create energy through photosynthesis. Producers form the base of every food chain.

text_segment: Animals are called consumers because they cannot make their own food. They must eat plants or other animals to get energy. Herbivores eat plants, carnivores eat meat, and omnivores eat both.

text_segment: Decomposers like mushrooms and bacteria break down dead plants and animals. They return nutrients to the soil so new plants can grow. Without decomposers, dead material would pile up everywhere.

text_segment: There are three main types of soil: sandy, clay, and loamy. Sandy soil drains water quickly but lacks nutrients. Clay soil holds water but can be too wet. Loamy soil mixes both and is best for plants.
question: Describe the three main types of soil.
Active: Sandy, clay, and loamy soils have different properties.
Progressive: Sandy, clay, and loamy soils are having different properties.
Passive: Different properties are found in sandy, clay, and loamy soils.
Subordinate: Because loamy soil drains well, plants grow best in it.

order_subordinate_beginner_2: plants grow,best in it,because,loamy soil,drains well,.

text_segment: Humus is dark, rich soil made from decayed plants and animals. It contains many nutrients that plants need to grow. Gardeners add compost to soil to increase humus and help plants grow better.

Progressive: Humus is containing nutrients that help plants grow.
Passive: Nutrients are contained in humus to help plants.

text_segment: There are three main types of rocks. Igneous rocks form when melted rock cools. Sedimentary rocks form from layers of sand and mud. Metamorphic rocks form when other rocks change under heat and pressure.

text_segment: Leave No Trace teaches us to protect nature when we visit outdoor places. The seven principles help us enjoy nature without harming it. Following these rules keeps wild places beautiful for everyone.

Progressive: Leave No Trace is teaching people to protect natural areas.

text_segment: Plan ahead and prepare is the first Leave No Trace principle. Know the rules for the area you will visit. Bring the right gear and check the weather before your trip.

text_segment: Pack it in, pack it out means taking all your trash home with you. Do not leave garbage, food scraps, or litter in nature. Pick up any trash you find, even if it is not yours.

text_segment: Travel on durable surfaces means staying on trails and paths. Walking off trails damages plants and causes erosion. Camp on hard ground or established campsites to protect the soil.

text_segment: Respect wildlife by observing animals from a distance. Never feed wild animals because it can make them sick. Feeding also makes animals depend on humans instead of finding natural food.

text_segment: Ecological succession is how natural communities change over time. After a disturbance like a fire or flood, plants and animals slowly return. Different species appear in stages until a stable community forms.

Active: Succession describes how natural communities change over time.
Progressive: Succession is describing how communities change over time.
Passive: How communities change is described by succession.
Subordinate: After a disturbance, different species return in stages.

order_subordinate_beginner_2: different species,return in stages,after,a disturbance,.

text_segment: Pioneer species are the first plants to grow after a disturbance. They include grasses, mosses, and small wildflowers. These tough plants prepare the soil for larger plants that come later.

Passive: Soil is prepared by pioneer species for other plants.

order_subordinate_beginner_2: pioneer species,grow first,after,fires clear land,.

text_segment: A climax community is the final stable stage of succession. In forests, this might be a community of mature oak or pine trees. The climax community stays the same unless a major disturbance occurs.
